text.title: Experimentation at Scale Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Experimentation at Scale plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Every experiment MUST pre-register hypothesis, primary metric, MDE, sample-size target, and guardrail metrics before traffic is allocated; post-hoc metric selection is forbidden.

rationale.source: Kohavi, Tang, Xu — Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments (2020)

rule.rationale: Post-hoc metric picking is p-hacking; pre-registration is the only structural gate against it at scale.

rule.statement: Every experiment MUST run a Sample Ratio Mismatch (SRM) chi-square check before reading results; SRM-failing experiments MUST be discarded, not patched.

rationale.source: Kohavi et al. — SRM at Microsoft (2018)

rule.rationale: SRM failure means traffic allocation is broken; any 'lift' read off an SRM-failing experiment is an artefact, not an effect.

rule.statement: Guardrail metrics (latency, error-rate, revenue per session) MUST be defined per surface and MUST block shipping when degraded by more than the pre-registered threshold.
rule.rationale: Without guardrails, primary-metric wins ship while collateral damage (slower pages, broken flows) goes undetected.

rule.statement: Every concluded experiment MUST produce a one-page learning artefact (hypothesis, result, decision, follow-ups); experiments without a learning page MUST be re-opened.
rule.rationale: Without an extraction step, organisations run thousands of experiments and accumulate zero institutional memory; the playbook becomes the read-only result dashboard.

rule.statement: Experiments MUST run to the pre-registered sample size; early-stopping for 'looks significant' is forbidden unless a sequential test (mSPRT, group-sequential) was pre-registered.

rationale.source: Johari et al. — Peeking at A/B Tests (2017)

rule.rationale: Peeking inflates false-positive rate above the nominal alpha; the only safe early stop is a pre-registered sequential design.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
